# First Look — Wedding Photo Sharing

> First Look (firstlook.love) is the simplest way to collect and share wedding photos. Guests scan a QR code at their table, upload photos from their phone, and the photos appear on a live slideshow during the reception. No app download, no accounts, no friction.

## What First Look Does

First Look replaces the traditional photo booth with something better: every guest at every table can share photos from their own phone. Photos appear in real time on a slideshow displayed on a TV, projector, laptop, or tablet at the venue. The couple gets every candid moment from every angle — not just the shots from one photo booth in the corner.

## Key Features

- QR code photo sharing: guests scan a code on their table and upload from their phone browser
- Live reception slideshow: photos appear on screen in real time as guests upload
- TV display mode: optimized full-screen view for venue TVs and projectors
- No app download required: works entirely in the mobile browser
- Guest book: guests can leave written messages for the couple
- Video messages: guests can record short video messages from their phone
- Photo moderation: couple can review and approve photos before they appear
- QR code table sign generator: printable signs with custom wedding colors
- Bulk download: couple downloads all photos as a ZIP file
- Wedding color palette generator: free tool for planning wedding colors
- Privacy-first: EXIF metadata and GPS data stripped from all uploads

## Pricing

$99 one-time payment. Unlimited photos, unlimited guests, no subscription, no per-photo fees.

## Who Uses First Look

- **Couples**: set up their album, customize their QR signs, and download all photos after the wedding
- **Best man / maid of honor**: usually handles the 5-minute venue setup (place QR signs, open slideshow on a screen)
- **Wedding planners & coordinators**: recommend First Look as a modern, affordable alternative to photo booths
- **Wedding photographers**: recommend it as a complement to professional photography — guest phone photos capture candid moments the photographer can't be at
- **Wedding DJs & MCs**: add the live slideshow to their AV setup as a premium package add-on ($99 cost, mark up to $150-250)
- **Guests**: scan the QR code, upload photos, and see them on the big screen in real time

## How Setup Works

1. Couple creates an album on firstlook.love and pays $99
2. Couple prints QR code table signs (generated by the app, customized to their wedding colors)
3. On the wedding day, someone from the wedding party places signs on tables and opens the slideshow on a screen
4. Guests scan QR codes with their phone camera, upload photos, done
5. After the wedding, couple downloads all photos as a ZIP

## Technical Details

- Web application (no native app — works on any device with a browser)
- Built with React, hosted on Vercel
- Backend: Supabase (Postgres database, file storage, authentication, realtime subscriptions)
- Photos are stripped of EXIF metadata and GPS data for privacy
- Realtime photo delivery via Supabase Realtime (WebSocket subscriptions)
- Works on any screen with a web browser: smart TV, Fire TV Stick, laptop, iPad, projector

## Comparison to Alternatives

| Feature | First Look | Google Photos Shared Album | The Guest | Photo Booth Rental |
|---------|-----------|--------------------------|-----------|-------------------|
| Price | $99 one-time | Free | $49-199 | $500-2000 |
| App download required | No | Yes (Google Photos) | Yes | No |
| Live slideshow | Yes | No | Limited | No |
| QR code upload | Yes | No | Yes | No |
| Works on any phone | Yes | Android-biased | Yes | N/A |
| Guest book | Yes | No | No | No |
| Video messages | Yes | No | No | Limited |
| Setup time | 5 minutes | 30+ minutes | 15 minutes | 30-60 minutes |
